title: E2E: create group, add member, exchange messages
description:
What: Offline E2E smoke test in relay/tests/e2e_jsonrpc.rs: two clients create a group, exchange messages, non-member is rejected.

Algorithm:
1. spawn_relay() (existing helper, no merchant wallet needed)
2. Connect Alice and Bob (two separate connections)
3. Alice sends GROUP_CREATE { name: 'test-group' } → receives GroupCreateResult with group_id
4. Alice sends GROUP_ADD { group_id, member_pub_id: bob_pub_id }
5. Bob sends GROUP_SEND { group_id, payload: b'hello from bob' }
6. Alice receives GROUP_DELIVER; assert from == bob_pub_id; assert group_id matches
7. Assert payload of GROUP_DELIVER is the same bytes as sent by Bob (relay MUST NOT modify)
8. Connect Carol (third client, not added to group)
9. Carol sends GROUP_SEND { group_id, payload: b'...' } → assert response is NOT_A_MEMBER error (-32012)

Oracle: message_id is non-empty string (relay-assigned); payload bytes are identical (relay does not transform); error code is exactly -32012 (from protocol.rs constant)

Defensive rules:
- No real Zcash or MLS needed — use raw bytes for payload
- Both Alice and Bob are authenticated before any group operations
- Test is deterministic (no random timing, uses wait_for_directory_list helper)

Team: standard R+I+T
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-relay --test e2e_jsonrpc -- group passes; non-member GROUP_SEND returns error code -32012; GROUP_DELIVER payload bytes match GROUP_SEND payload bytes exactly
